basic_details.job_title: MoEFCC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.organization_name: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)
basic_details.short_information: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) Recruitment 2026: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has released the official notification for 1 Young Professional (Technical) posts, inviting applications from eligible candidates. This recruitment provides an excellent opportunity for candidates seeking government jobs.

qualification.education_qualification: Master’s degree in Environmental studies, Climate Change, Development studies or related fields OR B.Tech. in Environmental Engineering or related fields.
qualification.details: Master’s degree from a recognized institution in Environmental studies, Climate Change, Development studies or related fields. B.Tech. in Environmental Engineering or related fields. Relevant experience (post-qualification) in climate change and related fields: More than 3 years and up to 5 years.

selection_process: Shortlisting of applications by UNDP in association with NATCOM Cell Interview by Selection Committee
how_to_apply: Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.
Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email.
Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification.
Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 27-05-2026.
Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the MoEFCC Young Professional (Technical) recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your application via email is 27th May 2026. Ensure your application reaches the concerned authority before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the Young Professional (Technical) post?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Master’s degree in Environmental studies, Climate Change, Development studies or related fields, or a B.Tech. in Environmental Engineering or related fields.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are available for the Young Professional (Technical) position?
faq.answer: There is one vacancy available for the Young Professional (Technical) post in the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for candidates applying for the Young Professional (Technical) post is 32 years.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Young Professional (Technical) role?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 70,000/- per month.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application mode for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Applications are to be submitted via email. Candidates need to send their complete application along with required documents to the specified email address.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee required for any category of candidates applying for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for the Young Professional (Technical) post?
faq.answer: The selection process involves shortlisting of applications by UNDP in association with NATCOM Cell, followed by an interview conducted by a Selection Committee.

faq.question: Q9. What type of job is this recruitment?
faq.answer: This is a contract-based job opportunity for the Young Professional (Technical) position.

faq.question: Q10. What is the required experience for the Young Professional (Technical) post?
faq.answer: Candidates need to have relevant post-qualification experience in climate change and related fields, ranging from more than 3 years up to 5 years.
